teaching_card.title: Pronoun-Antecedent Agreement (Easy)
teaching_card.body: A pronoun must agree in number with the noun it refers to (its antecedent). A singular antecedent takes a singular pronoun; a plural antecedent takes a plural one.
teaching_card.worked_example: The company released its (not their) report.

screens.title: Agree in number
screens.body: A pronoun must match the noun it stands for (its antecedent) in number. A singular antecedent takes a singular pronoun (its, his, her); a plural antecedent takes a plural one (their).

screens.title: Find the real antecedent
screens.body: Look past phrases that come between the noun and the pronoun. 'The company, along with its partners,' is still singular — 'company,' not 'partners,' is the antecedent. Collective nouns (team, committee) are usually singular.

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: 'The committee, along with the regional offices, released ___ report.' The antecedent is 'committee' (singular), so 'its' — the plural 'offices' is bait. Note: 'it's' means 'it is,' never possession.
screens.worked_example: The players released THEIR statement; the team released ITS.
